title: [LowerFirMem] Insert memory modules before first user
body:
Currently the `LowerFirMem` pass inserts `HWModuleGeneratedOp`s all the way at the top of the file. This is problematic since passes like FIRRTL's `LowerToHW` will produce an SV preamble with macro definitions that the memories want to rely on. Inserting the memory modules at the top of the file puts them before the macro definitions that they rely on.

This commit changes the insertion point of memory modules, making them appear as late as possible in the IR, basically just before the first module that instantiates the memory. This ensures that the memory modules are always placed after that preamble and makes their location more logical within the IR.
